title: Refract system architecture record
summary: A supporting architecture view of the Refract implementation across the frontend, backend, database, vector store, and file storage layers.

links.label: Repository
links.description: Source repository for the architecture diagram.

pageSections.title: A technical view of the application boundaries
pageSections.markdown: Refract coordinates several forms of state at once: application data, uploaded documents, vector retrieval, and analytical outputs. The architecture diagram makes those layers explicit and shows how they are separated without losing the continuity provided by the session model.

pageSections.title: The architecture reflects the product’s split responsibilities
pageSections.description: The frontend handles research-facing interaction, the backend coordinates services and workflow logic, and storage layers preserve documents, state, and retrieval context. The system is easiest to understand when these responsibilities are visible at the same time.
pageSections.caption: Architecture diagram from the Refract repository README.

pageSections.title: The important seam is the shared research state
pageSections.markdown: A diagram can make a system look more complicated than it is, or it can reveal what actually matters. In this case, the most important seam is not simply that the app has a frontend and a backend. It is that the research state must remain coherent across document operations, evidence capture, comparison logic, and later statistical work. That is the requirement the architecture has to satisfy.
